item.title: Goldman Floats Risk Transfer Deal Tied to Private Market Loans
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is sounding out investors about a significant risk transfer tied to a portfolio of loans to private market funds.

item.title: Communist Cuba Is Leaning on Capitalists as Food and Oil Run Out
item.description: Private businesses that emerged on the island despite years of persecution will be key to whatever comes next as Trump pushes to end 67 years of one-party rule.

item.title: Revolut Offers All Staffers £1000 to Help Lure In New Business
item.description: Revolut Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Nik Storonsky is offering employees across the digital bank £1000 ($1,334) to woo more businesses to bank with Revolut, setting it as its highest priority to further boost the digital lender’s growth, valuation and revenue.

item.title: Colombian GDP Beats Expectations Ahead of Presidential Vote
item.description: Colombia’s economy grew faster than expected in the first quarter, potentially boosting President Gustavo Petro’s leftist ally in this month’s presidential vote.

item.title: UnitedHealth Tracks Workers’ AI Use in Push to Transform Company
item.description: UnitedHealth Group Inc. is tracking how often some employees use artificial intelligence tools as part of a push to embed the technology throughout its operations, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Advent to Sell Up to €1.5 Billion of InPost Buyout Loan to Banks
item.description: Advent International LP seeks to sell about a third of a €4.2 billion ($4.9 billion) buyout loan for Polish parcel-locker company InPost SA to a group of banks, before the rest is marketed to institutional investors.

item.title: Colombian Central Banker Says Interest Rates Need to Go Higher
item.description: Colombia’s central bank still has work to do to tame inflation, though may now be able to slow the pace of interest rate increases, according to co-director Bibiana Taboada.

item.title: Ford Set for Worst Day Since 2024 as AI-Driven Rally Abates
item.description: Ford Motor Co.’s blistering rally screeched to a halt Friday, as a broader market selloff cooled enthusiasm over the automaker’s potential to benefit from the boom in energy demand for artificial intelligence.

item.title: Bitcoin Dips Below $79,000 as Inflation Fears Hit Risk Assets
item.description: A selloff in risker assets like stocks swept up cryptocurrencies as concerns over inflation and high oil prices rattle jittery investors.

item.title: Warren Buffett, Steph Curry Lunch Auction Draws $9 Million Winning Bid
item.description: An anonymous bidder paid $9 million for lunch with Warren Buffett, National Basketball Association champion Stephen Curry and actress and entrepreneur Ayesha Curry.

item.title: Nigeria’s Oando Sees Iran War Windfall as Buyers Seek Safer Oil
item.description: Nigerian oil producer Oando Energy is witnessing a surge in revenue because of the Iran war, which has “shattered” the Gulf’s reputation as a safe place to operate, said Group Chief Executive Wale Tinubu.

item.title: SocGen Says Warsh Faces Big Test With Yields Turning ‘Unhinged’
item.description: A bond market spooked by fears of accelerating inflation will be an early test for incoming Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh, according to the head of research for Societe Generale Americas.

item.title: Pakistan to Borrow More From China’s Onshore Markets This Year
item.description: Pakistan is keen to raise more money from China’s onshore markets this year after the country successfully issued its debut yuan-denominated notes, according to Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb.

item.title: Modi Agrees With UAE to Build Strategic Crude, Gas Stockpiles
item.description: India will partner with the United Arab Emirates to expand its strategic crude and gas reserves, a key step for the world’s third-largest oil consumer to guard against future supply disruptions.

item.title: Thiel-Backed Erebor Offers Venezuela Unlikely Banking Lifeline
item.description: Peter Thiel-backed startup Erebor Bank has pitched itself to top Venezuelan officials on restoring the sanctioned country’s access to the US financial system, an unexpected play by a new firm that’s stepping into a politically sensitive market many global banks still avoid.

item.title: US Industrial Production Rises by Most in Over a Year
item.description: US industrial production rose in April by the most in over a year, pointing to nascent momentum in the sector.

item.title: Uber Rival Bolt, China’s Dongfeng Reach South Africa EV Deal
item.description: Bolt Technology OU agreed a deal with China’s Dongfeng Motor Group Co. to roll out an electric-vehicle fleet in South Africa that Yugo Rides will manage.

item.title: Nigeria Inflation Jumps Ahead of First Post-Iran War Rate Call
item.description: Nigeria’s annual inflation rate climbed to its highest level in five months as the fallout from the Iran war continued to fan energy prices, complicating the outlook for policymakers ahead of their first meeting since the conflict began.

item.title: Russia’s ‘Huge’ Spring Wheat Delays Risk Lowering Harvests
item.description: Heavy rains and colder-than-usual weather in Russia have resulted in the worst start to spring planting in many years, risking lower wheat harvests in the world’s top exporter of the grain.

item.title: Stock Futures Slide as Inflation Jitters Bring Rally to a Halt
item.description: Rising bond yields curbed traders’ appetite for risky bets early Friday, sending stocks lower following a weeks-long record-setting rally driven by a rush of cash into all things artificial intelligence.

item.title: GFL-Secure Deal Gets Proxy Firms’ Backing Despite Abrams’ Opposition
item.description: Two major proxy advisory firms backed GFL Environmental Inc.’s proposed acquisition of Secure Waste Infrastructure Corp., dealing a setback to Abrams Capital Management, the activist investor fighting the deal.

item.title: Hedge Fund Founder Wins £1.6 Million After Ex-Employee’s Abusive Discrimination Case
item.description: Claims of discrimination and harassment made against hedge fund founder Davide Leone were vexatious and had no reasonable prospect of success from the outset, a London judge ruled as he ordered Jacopo Moretti to pay over £1.6 million ($2.1 million) to his old boss.

item.title: Fidelity International’s Inflation Bet Pays Off as Bonds Slide
item.description: Even before the Iran war, Fidelity International portfolio manager Mike Riddell was skeptical of the view that global price pressures were subsiding. His contrarian bet — that inflation was set to rise — is now paying off handsomely.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 15, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 1% as of 7:48 a.m. in New York amid a broad global selloff in bonds.

item.title: Samsung Executives Make Rare Union Visit as Strike Fears Mount
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co.’s top management made a rare visit to leaders of the company’s largest labor union late Friday in an effort to revive stalled wage negotiations after government-mediated talks collapsed earlier this week.

item.title: Oil Demand Growth Faces Biggest Hit Since Covid on Iran Supply Shock
item.description: Major forecasters have slashed their expectations for consumption this year.

item.title: Trump Says He Discussed AI Guardrails, Nvidia’s Chips With Xi
item.description: US President Donald Trump said he discussed guardrails on artificial intelligence with Chinese leader Xi Jinping, while adding that Nvidia Corp.’s H200 chips also came up during a two-day summit in Beijing.

item.title: Aramco Cracks Open Its Empire to Wall Street in $35 Billion Push
item.description: Days after a BlackRock Inc.-led group signed an $11 billion lease agreement for some of Saudi Aramco’s natural gas facilities, the energy giant was inundated with calls from funds around the world eager for a slice of the business.

item.title: Germany’s Merz Says He Wouldn’t Advise His Children Work in US
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said that he wouldn’t recommend his children go to live in the US because of what he described as a “social climate” that has suddenly taken hold.

item.title: Disney Digs In Against Pressure From Trump’s FCC
item.description: The company is supporting ABC in a more spirited way than it did last year
